title: Invalid Dequeue Operation from Queue using Array

statement.markdown: Implement a Queue using an array. The queue should support enqueue and dequeue operations. If the queue is empty, dequeue operation should throw an error.
statement.inputFormat: The function will be called with a list of integers representing queue operations. The operations are as follows: 1 for enqueue, -1 for dequeue.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return a boolean indicating whether the operation was successful.
statement.notes: You can assume that the input will always be a list of integers. The first element of the list is the number of operations. The next elements are the operations themselves.

examples.explanation: Enqueue 2 into a 2-element queue that is full.

hints.text: Handle the edge case where the queue is empty before attempting to dequeue an element.

editorial.title: Queue Implementation with Array
editorial.intuition: Use an array to implement the queue. Enqueue operation adds elements at the end of the array. Dequeue operation removes elements from the front of the array.
editorial.bruteForce: Implement queue operations manually using array manipulation.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a circular array to implement the queue.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution has a time complexity of O(1) and space complexity of O(n).
editorial.dryRun: Queue operations: Enqueue(2), Dequeue(), Enqueue(3), Dequeue(), Dequeue()
commonMistakes: Not handling the edge case where the queue is empty before dequeueing an element

functionSignature.java: public boolean solve(int maxSize, int[] operations);
functionSignature.python: def solve(self, maxSize: int, operations: list[int]) -> bool:
